This Agreement is expected to be executed on or prior to the Pricing Date of March 19, 2025.

**[RECITALS]{.underline}**

**WHEREAS**, Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc., a Delaware corporation (the \"**Company**\"), with principal offices at 4200 Clearwater Blvd., Suite 800, Austin, Texas 78759, proposes to file a Registration Statement on Form S-1 (File No. 333-284517) with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the \"**Commission**\") under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the \"**Securities Act**\"), relating to the initial public offering (the \"**Offering**\") of shares of the Company\'s common stock, par value \$0.001 per share (the \"**Common Stock**\");

**WHEREAS**, the Offering will include the sale of shares of Common Stock by certain stockholders of the Company (each, a \"**Selling Stockholder**\"), as described in the Registration Statement and the related Prospectus;

**WHEREAS**, the undersigned Selling Stockholder (the \"**Stockholder**\") desires to sell shares of Common Stock in the Offering and wishes to appoint attorneys-in-fact and a custodian to act on the Stockholder\'s behalf in connection therewith;

**WHEREAS**, the underwriters of the Offering are expected to be led by Hargrove Securities LLC, as lead book-running manager, and Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc., as co-manager (collectively, the \"**Underwriters**\");

**WHEREAS**, the Stockholder desires to irrevocably appoint David Nishimura, Chief Financial Officer of the Company, and Samantha Reeves, General Counsel and Secretary of the Company, as the Stockholder\'s attorneys-in-fact and custodians for the purposes set forth herein; and

**WHEREAS, the Selling Stockholders consist of Cascade Kestridge Ventures, LP, a Delaware limited partnership, Northlight Growth Partners Fund II, LP, a Delaware limited partnership, and Dr. Anand Krishnamurthy, an individual, each of whom executes a separate copy of this Agreement.**

**NOW, THEREFORE**, in consideration of the premises and the mutual covenants and agreements contained herein, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the Stockholder hereby agrees as follows:

**[1. Appointment of Attorneys-in-Fact]{.underline}**

The Stockholder hereby irrevocably constitutes and appoints David Nishimura and Samantha Reeves, and each of them individually (the \"**Attorneys-in-Fact**\"), as the Stockholder\'s true and lawful attorneys-in-fact, with full power of substitution and resubstitution, for the Stockholder and in the Stockholder\'s name, place, and stead, to act on behalf of the Stockholder in connection with the Offering, with full power and authority to do and perform each and every act and thing requisite and necessary to be done in connection therewith, as fully and to all intents and purposes as the Stockholder might or could do in person.

This appointment constitutes a durable power of attorney coupled with an interest and shall not be revoked by the death, disability, or incapacity of the Stockholder, or, in the case of an entity, the dissolution or termination of the Stockholder. This appointment is irrevocable and shall survive and remain in full force and effect until the earlier of: (a) the completion of the Closing (and any Additional Closing relating to the exercise of the over-allotment option) under the Underwriting Agreement; or (b) the termination of the Underwriting Agreement without any sale of shares having occurred.

**[2. Scope of Authority]{.underline}**

The Stockholder hereby grants to the Attorneys-in-Fact full power and authority to take any and all of the following actions on behalf of and in the name of the Stockholder:

> \(a\) Execute and deliver, on behalf of and in the name of the Stockholder, the underwriting agreement (the \"**Underwriting Agreement**\") among the Company, the Selling Stockholders, and the Underwriters, in such form as the Attorneys-in-Fact shall approve;
>
> \(b\) Execute and deliver any amendments, supplements, or modifications to the Underwriting Agreement;
>
> \(c\) Execute and deliver any lock-up agreement or market standoff agreement required by the Underwriters in connection with the Offering, including a lock-up agreement providing for a 180-day restricted period from the date of the Final Prospectus (and, in the case of Dr. Anand Krishnamurthy, inclusive of the springing extension provision described in the applicable term sheet);
>
> \(d\) Deliver or cause to be delivered the shares of Common Stock to be sold by the Stockholder (the \"**Stockholder Shares**\") pursuant to the Underwriting Agreement, including by directing the Transfer Agent, Atlantic Stock Transfer & Trust Company, to transfer such shares in accordance with the terms of the Underwriting Agreement;
>
> \(e\) Receive and receipt for the net proceeds from the sale of the Stockholder Shares, after deduction of the underwriting discount and any applicable transfer taxes, and deposit such proceeds in accordance with the Stockholder\'s written instructions;
>
> \(f\) Execute and deliver selling stockholder questionnaires, certificates, and other documents required in connection with the Registration Statement, the Underwriting Agreement, or the Offering;
>
> \(g\) Furnish information in writing on behalf of the Stockholder for inclusion in the Registration Statement and Prospectus, subject to the Stockholder\'s prior written approval of the specific information to be furnished;
>
> \(h\) Determine the number of Stockholder Shares to be sold in the Offering, consistent with the maximum number specified in Schedule A hereto;
>
> \(i\) Execute, deliver, and file any documents required in connection with applicable federal, state, or foreign securities laws; and
>
> \(j\) Take all other actions and execute all other documents as may be necessary or desirable to consummate the sale of the Stockholder Shares in the Offering.

**[3. Custody of Shares]{.underline}**

The Stockholder hereby deposits with the Attorneys-in-Fact, acting as custodians (in such capacity, the \"**Custodians**\"), certificates or book-entry positions representing the Stockholder Shares, or agrees to cause such shares to be delivered to or held by the Custodians or the Transfer Agent (Atlantic Stock Transfer & Trust Company) for the benefit of the Custodians, promptly following execution of this Agreement.

The Custodians shall hold the Stockholder Shares in custody for the purposes of delivery to the Underwriters at the Closing (expected to be March 24, 2025, or such other date as determined pursuant to the Underwriting Agreement) and any Additional Closing related to the exercise of the over-allotment option.

If the Stockholder Shares are held in certificated form, the Stockholder shall deliver stock certificates endorsed in blank or accompanied by stock powers duly executed in blank, with signatures guaranteed, to the Custodians. If the Stockholder Shares are held in book-entry form, the Stockholder shall cause Atlantic Stock Transfer & Trust Company to hold such shares subject to the instructions of the Custodians.

The Custodians shall not release or deliver the Stockholder Shares except: (i) to the Underwriters at the Closing or any Additional Closing pursuant to the Underwriting Agreement; (ii) back to the Stockholder if the Underwriting Agreement is terminated prior to any Closing without any sale of shares having occurred; or (iii) as otherwise directed by the Stockholder and the Attorneys-in-Fact jointly in writing.

**[4. Number of Shares; Schedule A]{.underline}**

The maximum number of Stockholder Shares to be sold by the Stockholder in the Offering is as set forth on **Schedule A** attached hereto and incorporated herein by reference. The specific number of shares to be sold, the public offering price per share, the underwriting discount per share, and the net proceeds to the Stockholder per share shall be as set forth in the Underwriting Agreement as executed by the Attorneys-in-Fact on behalf of the Stockholder.

The Stockholder hereby authorizes the Attorneys-in-Fact to agree to a reduction, but not an increase, of the number of Stockholder Shares from the maximum number set forth on Schedule A, if necessary or appropriate in connection with the Offering. Schedule A sets forth the share information for each Selling Stockholder: Cascade Kestridge Ventures, LP --- up to 2,000,000 shares; Northlight Growth Partners Fund II, LP --- up to 1,500,000 shares; and Dr. Anand Krishnamurthy --- up to 500,000 shares.

**[5. Distribution of Proceeds]{.underline}**

Upon receipt of the net proceeds from the sale of the Stockholder Shares (calculated as the public offering price per share less the underwriting discount per share of \$1.44, less any applicable transfer taxes), the Custodians shall distribute such proceeds to the Stockholder in accordance with the Stockholder\'s written wire transfer instructions provided to the Custodians prior to the Closing Date.

The Custodians shall deduct from the Stockholder\'s net proceeds any transfer taxes, if any, payable by the Stockholder in connection with the sale of the Stockholder Shares; provided, however, that the Company has agreed, pursuant to the terms of the Offering, to reimburse transfer taxes attributable to Selling Stockholder shares, if any.

The Custodians shall have no obligation to invest or reinvest any proceeds held temporarily pending distribution to the Stockholder and shall not be liable for any interest or earnings on proceeds so held.

**[6. Representations and Warranties of the Stockholder]{.underline}**

The Stockholder hereby represents and warrants to the Attorneys-in-Fact and the Company as follows:

> \(a\) **Authority.** The Stockholder has full right, power, and authority to enter into this Agreement and to sell, assign, transfer, and deliver the Stockholder Shares. If the Stockholder is an entity, it is duly organized, validly existing, and in good standing under the laws of its jurisdiction of organization, and the execution and delivery of this Agreement and the consummation of the transactions contemplated hereby have been duly authorized by all necessary action on the part of the Stockholder.
>
> \(b\) **Title to Shares.** The Stockholder is the record and beneficial owner of the shares of Common Stock set forth on Schedule A, free and clear of all liens, encumbrances, equities, and claims, and has full right and authority to sell and deliver such shares. Upon delivery of and payment for the Stockholder Shares pursuant to the Underwriting Agreement, the Underwriters will receive valid title to such shares, free and clear of all liens, encumbrances, equities, and claims.
>
> \(c\) **No Conflicts.** The execution and delivery of this Agreement and the sale of the Stockholder Shares will not conflict with, result in a breach of, or constitute a default under: (i) any agreement, indenture, or instrument to which the Stockholder is a party or by which the Stockholder is bound; (ii) the organizational documents of the Stockholder (if an entity); or (iii) any order, judgment, or decree of any court or governmental authority having jurisdiction over the Stockholder.
>
> \(d\) **No Registration Rights Violation.** The sale of the Stockholder Shares does not and will not violate any registration rights agreement or similar agreement to which the Stockholder is a party.
>
> \(e\) **Selling Stockholder Information.** All information furnished or to be furnished by the Stockholder in writing expressly for use in the Registration Statement (File No. 333-284517), the Preliminary Prospectus, the Final Prospectus, or any amendment or supplement thereto is and will be true, correct, and complete in all material respects and does not and will not contain any untrue statement of a material fact or omit to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading.
>
> \(f\) **No Manipulation.** The Stockholder has not taken and will not take, directly or indirectly, any action designed to, or that might reasonably be expected to, cause or result in stabilization or manipulation of the price of the Common Stock in violation of applicable law.
>
> \(g\) **Compliance with Lock-Up.** The Stockholder will execute and deliver a lock-up agreement in the form required by the lead Underwriter, Hargrove Securities LLC, and will comply with all restrictions on transfer set forth therein for the duration of the applicable restricted period.
>
> \(h\) **No Brokers.** No broker, finder, or similar agent has been employed by or on behalf of the Stockholder in connection with the Offering, and no person is entitled to any commission, fee, or other compensation from the Stockholder in connection with the sale of the Stockholder Shares, other than the underwriting discount payable to the Underwriters.

**[7. Duties and Liabilities of Attorneys-in-Fact and Custodians]{.underline}**

The Attorneys-in-Fact and Custodians shall not be liable for any action taken or omitted to be taken in good faith in connection with this Agreement, except for their own willful misconduct or gross negligence. The Attorneys-in-Fact and Custodians are acting solely as agents of the Stockholder and shall have no fiduciary or other duty to the Stockholder beyond the specific duties expressly set forth in this Agreement. The Attorneys-in-Fact shall act in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and the Underwriting Agreement.

The Stockholder acknowledges that David Nishimura serves as Chief Financial Officer and Samantha Reeves serves as General Counsel and Secretary of the Company, and that each is also acting on behalf of the Company and other Selling Stockholders in connection with the Offering. The Stockholder hereby waives any conflict of interest arising therefrom. The Attorneys-in-Fact shall have no obligation to take any action that they reasonably believe to be contrary to applicable law.

**[8. Indemnification of Attorneys-in-Fact and Custodians]{.underline}**

The Stockholder agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Attorneys-in-Fact and Custodians from and against any and all losses, claims, damages, liabilities, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys\' fees and expenses) arising out of or relating to the performance of their duties under this Agreement, except to the extent that such losses, claims, damages, liabilities, or expenses result from the willful misconduct or gross negligence of the Attorneys-in-Fact or Custodians. This indemnification obligation shall survive the termination of this Agreement.

**[9. Irrevocability]{.underline}**

This Power of Attorney and Custody Agreement is coupled with an interest and is irrevocable. It shall not be revoked by the death, disability, incapacity, dissolution, liquidation, or termination of the Stockholder, or by any other event. This Agreement may be terminated only upon mutual written agreement of the Stockholder and the Attorneys-in-Fact, or automatically upon the earlier of: (a) the completion of all closings under the Underwriting Agreement, including any closing related to the exercise of the over-allotment option (the deadline for exercise of which is April 23, 2025); or (b) the termination of the Underwriting Agreement without any closing having occurred.

**[10. Successors and Assigns]{.underline}**

This Agreement shall be binding upon the Stockholder and the Stockholder\'s heirs, executors, administrators, legal representatives, successors, and assigns. This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of the Attorneys-in-Fact and their successors. The Attorneys-in-Fact may not assign their obligations hereunder without the prior written consent of the Stockholder; provided, however, that the Company may designate a successor to serve in the capacity of Attorney-in-Fact if either David Nishimura or Samantha Reeves ceases to be an officer of the Company, upon written notice to the Stockholder.

**[11. Governing Law]{.underline}**

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to conflict-of-laws principles thereof. Any dispute arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the federal and state courts located in the Borough of Manhattan, City of New York, and each party hereby irrevocably consents to the personal jurisdiction of such courts.

**[12. Notices]{.underline}**

All notices, requests, demands, and other communications under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be delivered to the parties at the following addresses:

> If to the Attorneys-in-Fact / Custodians:
>
> c/o Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc. 4200 Clearwater Blvd., Suite 800 Austin, Texas 78759
>
> Attention: David Nishimura, Chief Financial Officer
>
> and Samantha Reeves, General Counsel
>
> If to the Stockholder:
>
> At the address set forth on the signature page hereto.

Notices shall be deemed given upon personal delivery, one (1) business day after deposit with a nationally recognized overnight courier service, or three (3) business days after mailing by certified or registered mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid.

**[13. Miscellaneous]{.underline}**

**Entire Agreement.** This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, understandings, negotiations, and discussions, whether oral or written, relating to such subject matter.

**Amendments.** This Agreement may not be amended, modified, or supplemented except by a written instrument signed by the Stockholder and the Attorneys-in-Fact.

**Severability.** If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable in any respect, such invalidity, illegality, or unenforceability shall not affect any other provision hereof, and the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.

**Counterparts.** This Agreement may be executed in one or more counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, and all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument. Electronic signatures and PDF copies of executed counterparts shall be treated as originals for all purposes.

**Headings.** Section headings contained in this Agreement are for convenience of reference only and shall not affect the meaning or interpretation of this Agreement.

**Third-Party Beneficiaries.** The Underwriters, Hargrove Securities LLC and Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc., are intended third-party beneficiaries of this Agreement with respect to the obligation of the Stockholder to deliver the Stockholder Shares and the authority of the Attorneys-in-Fact to execute the Underwriting Agreement on behalf of the Stockholder.

**\[SIGNATURE PAGES FOLLOW\]**

"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)

"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(f"pandoc failed: {result.stderr}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"OK: wrote {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) not in (3, 4):
        print("Usage: generate_from_md.py <input.md> <output.docx> [template.docx]", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(2)
    template = Path(sys.argv[3]) if len(sys.argv) == 4 else None
    generate(Path(sys.argv[1]), Path(sys.argv[2]), template)

"""Validate a .docx for delivery.

Checks ZIP integrity, XML well-formedness for every part, [Content_Types].xml
presence, and relationship consistency (no dangling rIds). Does NOT do XSD
schema validation in v1 — defer until ECMA-376 XSDs are vendored fresh from
ECMA-International.

Usage: python validate.py file.docx
Exit 0 = valid; non-zero = errors printed to stderr.
"""
import sys
import zipfile
from pathlib import Path
from lxml import etree


def validate(path: Path) -> list[str]:
    errors = []

    if not path.exists():
        return [f"File not found: {path}"]
    if not zipfile.is_zipfile(path):
        return [f"Not a valid ZIP: {path}"]

    with zipfile.ZipFile(path) as z:
        names = set(z.namelist())

        if "[Content_Types].xml" not in names:
            errors.append("Missing [Content_Types].xml")
            return errors

        # XML well-formedness for every .xml and .rels part
        for name in names:
            if name.endswith(".xml") or name.ends

**Firm-Commitment Initial Public Offering of Common Stock of Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc.**

Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc., a corporation duly organized and existing under the laws of the State of Delaware (the "Company"), the selling stockholders named in Schedule A hereto (collectively, the "Selling Stockholders," and each a "Selling Stockholder"), and Hargrove Securities LLC and Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc. (each an "Underwriter" and collectively, the "Underwriters"), for whom Hargrove Securities LLC and Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc. are acting as Representatives (in such capacity, the "Representatives"), hereby confirm their agreement with each other as follows:

## 1. Definitions

In addition to the terms defined elsewhere herein, the following terms have the meanings set forth in this Section 1:

"Act" means the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and the rules and regulations of the Commission thereunder.

"Exchange Act" means the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and the rules and regulations of the Commission thereunder.

"FINRA" means the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority, Inc.

"Firm Shares" means an aggregate of 12,000,000 shares of Common Stock, consisting of (i) 8,000,000 shares of Common Stock to be sold by the Company (the "Company Shares") and (ii) 4,000,000 shares of Common Stock to be sold by the Selling Stockholders (the "Selling Stockholder Shares"), in each case as set forth in Schedule A and Schedule B hereto.

"Free Writing Prospectus" means any "free writing prospectus" as defined in Rule 405 under the Act.

"Initial Prospectus" means the preliminary prospectus included in the Registration Statement at the Applicable Time.

"Lock-Up Agreements" means the lock-up agreements, substantially in the form of Exhibit A hereto, executed and delivered by the Company's directors, officers, and holders of one percent (1%) or more of the outstanding Common Stock (on a pre-Offering basis) pursuant to Section 5(o) hereof.

"Over-Allotment Option" means the option granted to the Underwriters pursuant to Section 2(b) hereof to purchase up to 1,800,000 Additional Shares.

"Preliminary Prospectus" means the preliminary prospectus included in the Registration Statement, dated March 12, 2025.

"Prospectus" means the Final Prospectus (as defined below).

"Public Offering Price" means $24.00 per share, being the initial public offering price per share at which the Firm Shares (and any Additional Shares) are to be offered to the public, as agreed among the Company, the Selling Stockholders, and the Representatives on the date hereof.

"Registration Statement" means the registration statement on Form S-1 (File No. 333-284517), as amended, including all documents incorporated or deemed to be incorporated by reference therein, filed by the Company with the Commission under the Act, as declared effective by the Commission on March 12, 2025.

"Regulation M" means Regulation M under the Exchange Act.

"Required Filings" means the filings required to be made by the Company with the Commission and the states in connection with the Offering.

"Rule 158," "Rule 164," "Rule 172," "Rule 405," "Rule 415," "Rule 424," "Rule 430A," "Rule 433" and "Rule 5110" mean, in each case, the rule under the Act or the Exchange Act, as applicable, so designated.

"Final Prospectus" means the final prospectus relating to the Firm Shares (and any Additional Shares) filed by the Company with the Commission pursuant to Rule 424(b)(4) under the Act.

"Testing-the-Waters Communication" means any "testing-the-waters communication" as defined in Rule 405 under the Act.

"Time of Sale" means the time at which the Firm Shares were first sold to the public pursuant to the Offering.

"Time of Sale Prospectus" means the Preliminary Prospectus, together with any Issuer Free Writing Prospectus and any other free writing prospectus permitted under Rule 164(b) under the Act, in the form used at the Time of Sale.

## 2. Sale and Purchase of Firm Shares; Over-Allotment Option

### (a) Firm Shares.

Upon the terms and subject to the conditions herein set forth, the Company agrees to sell to the several Underwriters, and each of the Underwriters, upon the several and not joint representations, warranties, covenants, and agreements of the Company and the Selling Stockholders contained herein, agrees to purchase from the Company and the Selling Stockholders, severally and not jointly, at the purchase price per share equal to the Public Offering Price less the underwriting discount of six percent (6.0%) (i.e., $1.44 per share, resulting in a purchase price of $22.56 per share) (the "Purchase Price"), the respective number of Firm Shares set forth opposite each Underwriter's name in Schedule B hereto.

### (b) Over-Allotment Option.

In order to facilitate the distribution of the Firm Shares, the Company hereby grants to the Representatives, for the account of the several Underwriters, an option (the "Over-Allotment Option") to purchase, severally and not jointly, up to 1,800,000 Additional Shares (equal to fifteen percent (15%) of the Firm Shares) from the Company, at the Purchase Price, solely for the purpose of covering over-allotments, if any, in connection with the Offering. The Over-Allotment Option is exercisable in whole or in part at any time, and from time to time, within thirty (30) calendar days after the Closing Date (i.e., on or before April 23, 2025), upon written or telegraphic notice to the Company by or on behalf of the Representatives. Upon any exercise of the Over-Allotment Option, the Additional Shares shall be purchased by the Underwriters severally and not jointly, in the respective proportions set forth in Schedule B hereto. The Additional Shares shall be purchased by the Underwriters from the Company only; the Selling Stockholders shall have no obligation to sell any Additional Shares, and the Over-Allotment Option shall be sourced exclusively from newly issued shares of Common Stock.

### (c) Additional Closing.

Upon any exercise of the Over-Allotment Option, the closing of the purchase of the Additional Shares (the "Additional Closing") shall take place at the offices of Ashford & Pine LLP, 1231 Avenue of the Americas, 34th Floor, New York, New York 10020, or remotely via the facilities of The Depository Trust Company, on the third business day after the date of the notice of exercise (or such other date as the Representatives and the Company may agree upon in writing).

## 3. Representations and Warranties of the Company

The Company represents and warrants to each Underwriter, as of the date hereof, the Applicable Time, and the Closing Date (or such other date as is expressly indicated), as follows:

### (a) Organization and Good Standing.

The Company is a corporation duly organized, validly existing, and in good standing under the laws of the State of Delaware and is qualified to do business as a foreign corporation in each jurisdiction in which the nature of its business or the ownership or leasing of its properties makes such qualification necessary, except where the failure to be so qualified would not, individually or in the aggregate, have a Material Adverse Effect (as defined below). "Material Adverse Effect" means any material adverse change in the business, properties, management, financial condition, or results of operations of the Company and its subsidiaries, taken as a whole.

### (b) Capitalization.

The authorized capital stock of the Company consists of 200,000,000 shares of Common Stock, par value $0.001 per share, and 10,000,000 shares of preferred stock, par value $0.001 per share. As of the date hereof, 42,000,000 shares of Common Stock are issued and outstanding, and no shares of preferred stock are issued and outstanding. All outstanding shares of Common Stock have been duly authorized, validly issued, fully paid, and nonassessable, and were issued in compliance with applicable federal and state securities laws. The Company's Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation, effective as of December 20, 2024, and its Amended and Restated Bylaws are in the form filed as exhibits to the Registration Statement. As of December 31, 2024, there were outstanding under the Company's 2024 Equity Incentive Plan 4,200,000 stock options at a weighted-average exercise price of $6.50 per share, and 1,800,000 shares of Common Stock were reserved for future grants thereunder.

### (c) Authorization; Enforceability.

The Company has full corporate power and authority to execute and deliver this Agreement and to consummate the transactions contemplated hereby. This Agreement has been duly authorized, executed, and delivered by the Company and constitutes the valid and binding obligation of the Company, enforceable against it in accordance with its terms, except as enforceability may be limited by applicable bankruptcy, insolvency, and similar laws and by general principles of equity.

### (d) Validity of the Shares.

The Company Shares and the Additional Shares, when issued and delivered and paid for in accordance with this Agreement, will be duly authorized, validly issued, fully paid, and nonassessable, and will be free of any liens, encumbrances, equities, or claims created by the Company.

### (e) No Conflicts.

The execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement and the consummation of the transactions contemplated hereby will not (i) conflict with or constitute a breach or default under the Company's Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation or Amended and Restated Bylaws; (ii) conflict with or constitute a default under any material agreement or instrument to which the Company is a party or by which it is bound, including the supply agreement with Tanaka Precision Components Ltd., the exclusive license agreement with the University of Texas at Austin, and the revolving credit facility with Oakvale National Bank; or (iii) violate any applicable law or order, in each case except where the conflict, breach, default, or violation would not, individually or in the aggregate, have a Material Adverse Effect.

### (f) Registration Statement and Prospectus.

The Registration Statement is the most recent registration statement filed by the Company with the Commission for the registration of the Firm Shares (and any Additional Shares) under the Act. The Registration Statement has been declared effective by the Commission under the Act. The Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, and the Final Prospectus, in each case as of the respective dates thereof and as of the Closing Date, comply and will comply in all material respects with the requirements of the Act and the Exchange Act and the rules and regulations thereunder, and do not and will not contain any untrue statement of a material fact or omit to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading. The documents incorporated or deemed to be incorporated by reference in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, and the Final Prospectus, when such documents were filed with the Commission, conformed and will conform in all material respects to the requirements of the Exchange Act and the rules and regulations thereunder and did not and will not, when read together with the other statements in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, or the Final Prospectus, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, contain any untrue statement of a material fact or omit to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein not misleading.

### (g) Financial Statements.

The financial statements included in the Registration Statement, together with the related notes and schedules, present fairly, in all material respects, the financial position of the Company and its subsidiaries as of the dates and for the periods indicated, and the results of operations and cash flows of the Company and its subsidiaries for the periods indicated, and such financial statements have been prepared in conformity with GAAP applied on a consistent basis throughout the periods involved. The supporting schedules, if any, included in the Registration Statement present fairly, in all material respects, the information required to be stated therein. The audited financial statements included in the Registration Statement have been audited by Whitman Reese & Co., independent registered public accounting firm, in accordance with the standards of the Public Company Accounting Oversight Board (United States).

### (h) No Material Adverse Change.

Since the date of the most recent audited financial statements included in the Registration Statement (December 31, 2024), (i) there has been no Material Adverse Effect and (ii) there has been no loss, destruction, or material damage to the properties of the Company and its subsidiaries, whether or not covered by insurance, that would, individually or in the aggregate, have a Material Adverse Effect.

### (i) Intellectual Property.

The Company owns or possesses sufficient legal rights to all patents, patent applications, trademarks, service marks, trade names, copyrights, domain names, trade secrets, know-how, and other intellectual property necessary for the conduct of its business as currently conducted and as described in the Registration Statement, including fourteen (14) issued U.S. utility patents, seven (7) pending patent applications, and the exclusive license agreement with the University of Texas at Austin for bioelectric signal processing technology. The Company has not received any written notice of infringement, conflict, or claim alleging that any of the foregoing is invalid or unenforceable, except as disclosed in the Registration Statement.

### (j) Regulatory Compliance.

The Company is in compliance in all material respects with all applicable laws, rules, and regulations of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration, including the requirements applicable to the PulseGuard Pro cardiac rhythm monitoring device, which received FDA 510(k) clearance (Clearance No. K230847) on August 22, 2023.

### (k) Material Contracts.

Each material contract to which the Company is a party, including the supply agreement with Tanaka Precision Components Ltd. (effective through December 31, 2027, with a minimum annual purchase commitment of $12,000,000), the exclusive license agreement with the University of Texas at Austin (effective July 1, 2018, with a remaining term through June 30, 2033, and a royalty of 3.5% of net sales), and the revolving credit facility with Oakvale National Bank (aggregate commitment of $40,000,000, of which $22,500,000 was outstanding as of December 31, 2024), is in full force and effect, constitutes a legal, valid, and binding obligation of the Company, and the Company is not in material breach or default thereunder.

### (l) Litigation.

Except as disclosed in the Registration Statement, there is no action, suit, proceeding, or investigation pending or, to the knowledge of the Company, threatened against the Company before any court or governmental authority that, if adversely determined, would, individually or in the aggregate, have a Material Adverse Effect. The matters disclosed in the Registration Statement include (i) the patent infringement action brought by VitaBand Corp. (Case No. 6:24-cv-00891, U.S. District Court, Eastern District of Texas), in which VitaBand Corp. seeks monetary damages of $45,000,000, plus enhanced damages, attorneys' fees, costs, and permanent injunctive relief, and (ii) a charge of discrimination filed with the U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission on September 3, 2024, with respect to which the Company has established a reserve of $350,000.

### (m) Tax Matters.

The Company has filed all material federal, state, and foreign tax returns required to be filed and has paid all material taxes due, except as disclosed in the Registration Statement. As of December 31, 2024, the Company had approximately $14.2 million in federal net operating loss carryforwards, as disclosed in the Registration Statement.

### (n) FINRA Affiliations.

No officer, director, or holder of five percent (5%) or more of the Company's securities (calculated on a pre-Offering basis) is, or within the 180 days prior to the initial filing of the Registration Statement has been, an affiliate or associated person of any FINRA member, except as disclosed in the Registration Statement or in the selling stockholder questionnaires delivered to the Representatives and Underwriters' Counsel.

### (o) Compliance with Securities Laws.

The Company has complied, and will comply, in all material respects with all applicable federal and state securities laws in connection with the Offering.

### (p) Nasdaq Listing.

The Firm Shares (and any Additional Shares) have been approved for listing on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the symbol "MPLS," subject to official notice of issuance.

### (q) No Stabilization.

The Company has not taken, and will not take, directly or indirectly, any action designed to cause or result in, or that would reasonably be expected to cause or result in, the stabilization or manipulation of the price of the Common Stock to facilitate the sale or resale of the Firm Shares (or any Additional Shares).

### (r) Independent Auditor.

Whitman Reese & Co., which has certified certain financial statements included in the Registration Statement, is an independent registered public accounting firm with respect to the Company within the meaning of the Act and the rules and regulations of the Commission and the Public Company Accounting Oversight Board.

### (s) Use of Proceeds.

The use of proceeds from the sale of the Company Shares, as described in the Registration Statement under "Use of Proceeds," does not and will not violate the provisions of Regulation T, U, or X of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System or any other applicable law.

### (t) Testing-the-Waters Communications.

The Company has not, without the prior written consent of the Representatives, made any Testing-the-Waters Communication other than Testing-the-Waters Communications with entities that are qualified institutional buyers or institutions that are accredited investors, in each case in compliance with Rule 163B under the Act.

## 4. Representations and Warranties of the Selling Stockholders

Each Selling Stockholder, severally and not jointly, represents and warrants to each Underwriter, as of the date hereof, the Applicable Time, and the Closing Date, as follows:

### (a) Title to Shares.

Such Selling Stockholder is the record and beneficial owner of, and has good, valid, and marketable title to, the Selling Stockholder Shares to be sold by it, free and clear of all liens, encumbrances, equities, and claims. Upon delivery of and payment for the Selling Stockholder Shares pursuant to this Agreement, the Underwriters will receive good, valid, and marketable title to such shares, free and clear of all liens, encumbrances, equities, and claims.

### (b) Authorization; Enforceability.

Such Selling Stockholder has full power and authority to execute and deliver this Agreement and the Power of Attorney and Custody Agreement appointing David Nishimura and Samantha Reeves as attorneys-in-fact, and to consummate the transactions contemplated hereby. This Agreement and the Power of Attorney and Custody Agreement have been duly authorized, executed, and delivered by such Selling Stockholder and constitute the valid and binding obligation of such Selling Stockholder, enforceable against it in accordance with its terms.

### (c) No Conflicts.

The execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement and the sale of the Selling Stockholder Shares by such Selling Stockholder will not conflict with, result in a breach or violation of, or constitute a default under, any agreement, instrument, or order to which such Selling Stockholder is a party or by which it is bound, or the organizational documents of such Selling Stockholder (if an entity).

### (d) Selling Stockholder Information.

All information furnished in writing by or on behalf of such Selling Stockholder specifically for inclusion in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, the Final Prospectus, or any amendment or supplement thereto (the "Selling Stockholder Information") is and will be, at the Applicable Time and as of the Closing Date, true, correct, and complete in all material respects and does not and will not contain any untrue statement of a material fact or omit to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading.

### (e) Compliance with Securities Laws.

Such Selling Stockholder has complied and will comply in all material respects with all applicable federal and state securities laws in connection with the Offering.

### (f) No Manipulation.

Such Selling Stockholder has not taken, and will not take, directly or indirectly, any action designed to cause or result in the stabilization or manipulation of the price of the Common Stock in violation of applicable law.

### (g) Lock-Up.

Such Selling Stockholder has executed and delivered, or will execute and deliver prior to the Closing Date, a Lock-Up Agreement in the form of Exhibit A hereto, and will comply with all restrictions set forth therein for the duration of the applicable Lock-Up Period.

### (h) Power of Attorney and Custody.

Such Selling Stockholder has executed and delivered the Power of Attorney and Custody Agreement appointing David Nishimura, Chief Financial Officer of the Company, and Samantha Reeves, General Counsel and Secretary of the Company, as its attorneys-in-fact and custodians for purposes of executing this Agreement, delivering the Selling Stockholder Shares at the Closing, and receiving and distributing the net proceeds therefrom.

## 5. Covenants of the Company and the Selling Stockholders

The Company covenants and agrees with each Underwriter as follows:

### (a) Filing of Final Prospectus.

The Company will file the Final Prospectus with the Commission pursuant to Rule 424(b)(4) under the Act within the time period required thereunder and will furnish to the Representatives copies of any amendment or supplement to the Final Prospectus promptly upon its filing.

### (b) Effectiveness of Registration Statement.

The Company will use its commercially reasonable efforts to keep the Registration Statement continuously effective under the Act until the later of (i) the Closing Date and (ii) the date on which all Additional Shares are purchased or the Over-Allotment Option expires, and will promptly notify the Representatives of any stop order or proceedings for a stop order and will use its commercially reasonable efforts to prevent the issuance of any stop order and to obtain the lifting of any such stop order if issued.

### (c) Blue Sky Qualifications.

The Company will use its commercially reasonable efforts to qualify the Firm Shares (and any Additional Shares) for sale under the Blue Sky laws of such jurisdictions as the Representatives may reasonably request and to maintain such qualifications for a period of one year from the Closing Date.

### (d) Delivery of Prospectus.

The Company will furnish to the Underwriters, without charge, such number of copies of the Preliminary Prospectus, the Final Prospectus, and any amendments or supplements thereto as the Representatives may reasonably request.

### (e) Reporting Requirements.

The Company will file all reports and other documents required to be filed by it under the Exchange Act within the periods specified therein.

### (f) Use of Proceeds.

The Company will apply the net proceeds from the sale of the Company Shares substantially as described in the Registration Statement under "Use of Proceeds," including the repayment of indebtedness under the revolving credit facility with Oakvale National Bank in accordance with the mandatory prepayment provisions of the credit agreement governing such facility.

### (g) Transfer Agent.

The Company will maintain a transfer agent and registrar for the Common Stock, being Atlantic Stock Transfer & Trust Company, satisfactory to the Representatives.

### (h) Listing.

The Company will use its commercially reasonable efforts to list the Firm Shares (and any Additional Shares) on the Nasdaq Global Select Market.

### (i) No Manipulation.

The Company will not, directly or indirectly, take any action designed to cause or result in the stabilization or manipulation of the price of the Common Stock.

### (j) Free Writing Prospectuses.

The Company will not, without the prior written consent of the Representatives, make any offer to sell the Firm Shares (or any Additional Shares) that would constitute a Free Writing Prospectus, other than (i) a Free Writing Prospectus that is an Issuer Free Writing Prospectus that has been reviewed and consented to by the Representatives or (ii) a Free Writing Prospectus that is permitted under Rule 164(b) under the Act.

### (k) Road Show.

The Company has conducted, or will conduct, a road show in compliance with Rule 163B and Rule 433 under the Act.

### (l) FINRA Filings.

The Company will cooperate with the Underwriters in connection with the filings required to be made with FINRA in connection with the Offering, including the filing required under FINRA Rule 5110, and will provide to the Representatives and Underwriters' Counsel all information reasonably requested in connection therewith.

### (m) Closing Certificate.

The Company will furnish to the Representatives, at the Closing Date, an officers' certificate signed by Dr. Anand Krishnamurthy, Chief Executive Officer, and David Nishimura, Chief Financial Officer, dated as of the Closing Date, in customary form, confirming the accuracy of the representations and warranties of the Company as of the Closing Date and the performance of the Company's covenants through the Closing Date.

### (n) Secretary's Certificate.

The Company will furnish to the Representatives, at the Closing Date, a secretary's certificate signed by Samantha Reeves, General Counsel and Secretary, dated as of the Closing Date, certifying as to the corporate resolutions authorizing the Offering and this Agreement, the Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation and Amended and Restated Bylaws, and the good standing of the Company.

### (o) Lock-Up Agreements.

The Company will use its commercially reasonable efforts to cause each of its directors, executive officers, and holders of one percent (1%) or more of the outstanding Common Stock (on a pre-Offering basis) to execute and deliver, prior to the Closing Date, a Lock-Up Agreement in the form of Exhibit A hereto. The Company will deliver to the Representatives, at or prior to the Closing Date, a list of all persons who have executed Lock-Up Agreements.

### (p) Press Release.

The Company will issue a press release announcing the pricing of the Offering promptly after the execution of this Agreement.

## 6. Public Offering Price; Delivery of and Payment for Shares

### (a) Public Offering Price.

The Public Offering Price is $24.00 per share, as agreed among the Company, the Selling Stockholders, and the Representatives on the date hereof. The underwriting discount is six percent (6.0%) of the Public Offering Price, or $1.44 per share, and the Purchase Price payable by the Underwriters to the Company and the Selling Stockholders is $22.56 per share.

### (b) Delivery and Payment.

Delivery of, and payment for, the Firm Shares shall be made at the offices of Ashford & Pine LLP, 1231 Avenue of the Americas, 34th Floor, New York, New York 10020, or remotely via the facilities of The Depository Trust Company, at 10:00 a.m., New York City time, on the Closing Date (March 24, 2025). Payment shall be made to the Company and the Selling Stockholders by wire transfer of immediately available funds to the accounts designated by the Company and the Selling Stockholders, respectively, against delivery of the Firm Shares to the Underwriters through the facilities of The Depository Trust Company for the accounts of the Underwriters.

### (c) Net Proceeds.

The aggregate net proceeds to the Company from the sale of the Company Shares (before offering expenses) shall be $180,480,000 (8,000,000 shares × $22.56). The aggregate net proceeds to the Selling Stockholders from the sale of the Selling Stockholder Shares (before any transfer taxes) shall be $90,240,000 (4,000,000 shares × $22.56), allocated as set forth in Schedule A hereto. If the Over-Allotment Option is exercised in full, the aggregate net proceeds to the Company from the sale of the Additional Shares shall be $40,608,000 (1,800,000 shares × $22.56).

## 7. Conditions to the Obligations of the Underwriters

The obligations of the several Underwriters to purchase and pay for the Firm Shares (and any Additional Shares) shall be subject to the accuracy, as of the date hereof, the Applicable Time, and the Closing Date, of the representations and warranties of the Company and the Selling Stockholders, to the performance by the Company and the Selling Stockholders of their covenants, and to the following additional conditions:

### (a) Registration Statement Effective.

The Registration Statement shall be effective under the Act at the Applicable Time and at the Closing Date, and no stop order suspending the effectiveness of the Registration Statement shall have been issued and no proceedings for that purpose shall have been initiated or threatened by the Commission.

### (b) Filing of Final Prospectus.

The Final Prospectus shall have been filed with the Commission pursuant to Rule 424(b)(4) under the Act within the time period required thereunder.

### (c) Comfort Letter.

The Representatives shall have received from Whitman Reese & Co., dated as of the date hereof (March 19, 2025), a comfort letter in form and substance satisfactory to the Representatives and Underwriters' Counsel, and a bring-down comfort letter dated as of the Closing Date (March 24, 2025), in form and substance satisfactory to the Representatives and Underwriters' Counsel, confirming, among other things, the matters set forth in the comfort letter delivered at pricing.

### (d) Legal Opinions.

The Representatives shall have received (i) a legal opinion and negative assurance letter from Stonebridge & Calloway LLP, Company Counsel, dated as of the Closing Date, and (ii) a legal opinion from Ashford & Pine LLP, Underwriters' Counsel, dated as of the Closing Date, in each case in form and substance satisfactory to the Representatives.

### (e) Officers' Certificate.

The Representatives shall have received the officers' certificate required by Section 5(m) hereof, dated as of the Closing Date.

### (f) Secretary's Certificate.

The Representatives shall have received the secretary's certificate required by Section 5(n) hereof, dated as of the Closing Date.

### (g) Lock-Up Agreements.

The Representatives shall have received executed Lock-Up Agreements from each of the Company's directors, executive officers, and holders of one percent (1%) or more of the outstanding Common Stock (on a pre-Offering basis), in the form of Exhibit A hereto.

### (h) Nasdaq Listing.

The Firm Shares (and any Additional Shares) shall have been approved for listing on the Nasdaq Global Select Market, subject to official notice of issuance.

### (i) No Material Adverse Change.

Since the date of the most recent audited financial statements included in the Registration Statement, there shall not have occurred any Material Adverse Effect.

### (j) FINRA Clearance.

The Underwriting Compensation shall have been cleared by FINRA, and the Underwriters shall have received no objection from FINRA with respect to the fairness or reasonableness of the underwriting arrangements. The total underwriting compensation in connection with the Offering shall not exceed eight percent (8.0%) of the gross offering proceeds, in compliance with FINRA Rule 5110, and no FINRA-disqualifying conflict of interest exists under FINRA Rule 5121.

### (k) Power of Attorney and Custody Agreements.

The Representatives shall have received executed Power of Attorney and Custody Agreements from each Selling Stockholder, appointing David Nishimura and Samantha Reeves as attorneys-in-fact and custodians.

### (l) Selling Stockholder Questionnaires.

The Representatives and Underwriters' Counsel shall have received completed selling stockholder questionnaires from each Selling Stockholder, in form and substance satisfactory to the Representatives and Underwriters' Counsel.

### (m) Additional Documents.

The Representatives shall have received such further information, certificates, and documents as they may reasonably request to enable them to pass upon the matters set forth in this Section 7.

## 8. Indemnification and Contribution

### (a) Indemnification by the Company.

The Company shall indemnify and hold harmless each Underwriter, its affiliates, and each person who controls such Underwriter within the meaning of Section 15 of the Act or Section 20 of the Exchange Act, against any and all losses, claims, damages, and liabilities (including reasonable costs of investigation and defense), joint or several, to which such indemnified party may become subject, arising out of or based upon any untrue statement or alleged untrue statement of a material fact contained in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, the Final Prospectus, any Issuer Free Writing Prospectus, or any amendment or supplement thereto, or any omission or alleged omission to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading; provided, however, that the Company shall not be liable to the extent that any such loss, claim, damage, or liability arises out of or is based upon any untrue statement or omission made in reliance upon and in conformity with written information furnished to the Company by or on behalf of any Underwriter specifically for use in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, the Final Prospectus, or any amendment or supplement thereto.

### (b) Indemnification by the Selling Stockholders.

Each Selling Stockholder shall, severally and not jointly, indemnify and hold harmless each Underwriter, its affiliates, and each person who controls such Underwriter within the meaning of Section 15 of the Act or Section 20 of the Exchange Act, against any and all losses, claims, damages, and liabilities to which such indemnified party may become subject, arising out of or based upon any untrue statement or alleged untrue statement of a material fact contained in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, the Final Prospectus, or any amendment or supplement thereto, or any omission or alleged omission to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading, but only with respect to the Selling Stockholder Information furnished in writing by such Selling Stockholder specifically for inclusion therein. Each Selling Stockholder's liability under this Section 8(b), whether for indemnification or contribution, shall not exceed the net proceeds actually received by such Selling Stockholder from the sale of its Selling Stockholder Shares in the Offering.

### (c) Indemnification by the Underwriters.

Each Underwriter shall, severally and not jointly, indemnify and hold harmless the Company, its directors, each officer of the Company who signed the Registration Statement, each person who controls the Company within the meaning of Section 15 of the Act or Section 20 of the Exchange Act, and each Selling Stockholder, against any and all losses, claims, damages, and liabilities arising out of or based upon any untrue statement or alleged untrue statement of a material fact contained in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, the Final Prospectus, or any amendment or supplement thereto, or any omission or alleged omission to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading, but only to the extent that such untrue statement or omission was made in reliance upon and in conformity with written information furnished to the Company by or on behalf of such Underwriter specifically for use therein.

### (d) Contribution.

If the indemnification provided for in this Section 8 is unavailable or insufficient to hold harmless an indemnified party, then each indemnifying party shall contribute to the aggregate amount of losses, claims, damages, and liabilities to which such indemnified party may be subject (i) in such proportion as is appropriate to reflect the relative benefits received by the Company, the Selling Stockholders, and the Underwriters from the Offering, or (ii) if such allocation is not permitted by applicable law, in such proportion as is appropriate to reflect not only such relative benefits but also the relative fault of the Company, the Selling Stockholders, and the Underwriters. The aggregate contribution of the Underwriters shall not exceed the total underwriting discount actually received by the Underwriters. No Selling Stockholder shall be required to contribute an amount in excess of the net proceeds received by such Selling Stockholder from the sale of its Selling Stockholder Shares in the Offering. No person found guilty of fraudulent misrepresentation within the meaning of Section 11(f) of the Act shall be entitled to contribution from any person who was not guilty of such fraudulent misrepresentation.

### (e) Procedure.

Promptly after receipt by an indemnified party of notice of the commencement of any action, such indemnified party shall, if a claim in respect thereof is to be made against an indemnifying party, notify the indemnifying party in writing; provided, however, that the failure to so notify shall not relieve the indemnifying party from any liability hereunder except to the extent it is materially prejudiced thereby. The indemnifying party shall be entitled to participate in, and, to the extent it shall desire, to assume the defense of any such action with counsel satisfactory to the indemnified party. The indemnified party shall have the right to employ separate counsel in any such action and to participate in the defense thereof, the fees and expenses of which shall be borne by the indemnified party unless the employment of such counsel has been authorized in writing by the indemnifying party in connection with the defense of such action or the indemnifying party has not employed counsel within a reasonable time after receiving notice of the action.

## 9. Termination

The Representatives may terminate this Agreement at any time prior to the Closing Date by written notice to the Company and the Selling Stockholders if, in the judgment of the Representatives, any of the following has occurred:

### (a) Material Adverse Change.

Any Material Adverse Change, or any event that, in the reasonable judgment of the Representatives, makes it impracticable or inadvisable to proceed with the Offering.

### (b) Market Disruption.

Any suspension or material limitation of trading in securities generally on the Nasdaq Stock Market, the New York Stock Exchange, or any other national securities exchange.

### (c) Banking Moratorium.

Any general moratorium on commercial banking activities declared by federal or New York state authorities.

### (d) Settlement Disruption.

Any material disruption in securities settlement, payment, or clearance services in the United States.

### (e) National Emergency.

Any outbreak or escalation of hostilities, declaration of war, national emergency, act of terrorism, or other calamity or crisis that, in the reasonable judgment of the Representatives, materially and adversely affects United States financial markets.

### (f) Rating Downgrade.

Any downgrade, or public notice of any intended or potential downgrade, of the rating of any securities of the Company or any of its subsidiaries by any nationally recognized statistical rating organization.

Upon any such termination, no party shall have any liability to any other party except (i) for expenses as provided in Section 10(g) and (ii) the indemnification and contribution provisions of Section 8, which shall survive any such termination.

## 10. Miscellaneous

### (a) Governing Law.

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to the conflict of laws principles thereof that would require the application of the laws of any other jurisdiction.

### (b) Jurisdiction; Waiver of Jury Trial.

Any dispute, claim, or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts located in the Borough of Manhattan, City of New York, State of New York. Each party hereby irrevocably submits to the personal jurisdiction of such courts and waives any objection to venue therein. Each party hereby irrevocably waives, to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, any and all rights to a trial by jury in any action arising out of or relating to this Agreement.

### (c) Notices.

All notices hereunder shall be in writing and shall be deemed duly given when delivered by hand, by nationally recognized overnight courier, or by email (with confirmation of receipt), to the parties at the following addresses:

### (d) Entire Agreement.

This Agreement, together with the Schedules and Exhibits hereto, constitutes the entire agreement among the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements, understandings, and negotiations, whether written or oral, with respect thereto.

### (e) Amendments; Waivers.

This Agreement may not be amended, modified, or supplemented except by a written instrument signed by the Company, the Selling Stockholders (or their attorneys-in-fact pursuant to the Power of Attorney and Custody Agreements), and the Representatives. No waiver of any provision hereof shall be effective unless in writing and signed by the party against whom the waiver is to be enforced.

### (f) Counterparts.

This Agreement may be executed in one or more counterparts (including by facsimile or portable document format (.pdf) signature pages), each of which shall be deemed an original, and all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument.

### (g) Expenses.

The Company shall bear all offering expenses, including SEC registration fees, FINRA filing fees, Nasdaq listing fees, printing and engraving expenses, transfer agent fees, Blue Sky qualification fees, road show expenses, Company counsel fees (Stonebridge & Calloway LLP), and accounting and auditing fees (Whitman Reese & Co.), estimated at approximately $3,200,000 in the aggregate. The Underwriters shall bear their own counsel fees and out-of-pocket expenses, including the fees and expenses of Underwriters' Counsel (Ashford & Pine LLP), estimated at approximately $850,000. Each Selling Stockholder shall bear its own legal counsel fees. The Company shall reimburse any transfer taxes attributable to the Selling Stockholder Shares, if any.

### (h) Survival.

The representations, warranties, covenants, and agreements contained herein, and the indemnification and contribution provisions of Section 8, shall survive the Closing and the delivery of and payment for the Firm Shares (and any Additional Shares).

### (i) Severability.

If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, the validity, legality, and enforceability of the remaining provisions shall not be affected.

### (j) Headings.

Section headings herein are for convenience of reference only and shall not affect the interpretation of this Agreement.

### (k) No Third-Party Beneficiaries.

Except for the indemnified parties expressly described in Section 8, this Agreement is not intended to, and shall not, create any rights or obligations in favor of any person or entity other than the parties hereto.

### (l) Selling Stockholder Execution.

Each Selling Stockholder has executed this Agreement, or caused this Agreement to be executed on its behalf by its attorneys-in-fact pursuant to the Power of Attorney and Custody Agreement, and the Selling Stockholders shall be bound by this Agreement severally and not jointly.

---

If the foregoing is in accordance with your understanding, kindly sign and return to the Company and the Selling Stockholders counterparts hereof, whereupon this Agreement, together with all counterparts, shall become a binding agreement among the parties in accordance with its terms.

## SCHEDULE C — LOCK-UP PARTIES

The following persons are required to execute and deliver Lock-Up Agreements in the form of Exhibit A hereto prior to the Closing Date, being all directors, executive officers, and holders of one percent (1%) or more of the outstanding Common Stock (on a pre-Offering basis of 42,000,000 shares):

## EXHIBIT A — FORM OF LOCK-UP AGREEMENT

[The form of Lock-Up Agreement is substantially in the form of the Lock-Up Agreement dated as of March __, 2025, among each locked-up party and Hargrove Securities LLC and Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc., as Representatives, providing for a lock-up period of one hundred eighty (180) days from the date of the Final Prospectus, with the customary exceptions set forth therein, including: (a) bona fide gifts and charitable contributions; (b) transfers to immediate family members or estate planning trusts; (c) transfers by operation of law, including pursuant to a qualified domestic relations order, divorce decree, or intestacy; (d) transfers to affiliates, limited partners, members, or stockholders of the locked-up party; (e) transactions pursuant to a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan established prior to the date of the Underwriting Agreement; (f) the exercise of stock options or settlement of restricted stock units (with the underlying shares remaining subject to the lock-up), including sell-to-cover transactions solely to satisfy tax withholding obligations; (g) the sale of shares pursuant to the Underwriting Agreement by a Selling Stockholder; and (h) transfers to the Company in connection with repurchases upon termination of employment or net exercise of options. In each case, the transferee must agree in writing to be bound by the terms of the Lock-Up Agreement.

Hargrove Securities LLC may, in its sole discretion, release any or all of the Lock-Up Securities at any time. If Hargrove Securities LLC releases Lock-Up Securities representing, in the aggregate, more than one percent (1%) of the Company's then-outstanding shares of Common Stock, Hargrove Securities LLC shall provide the Company with at least three (3) business days' prior written notice of such release, and the Company shall promptly announce such release by press release or on Form 8-K.]

## EXHIBIT B — SPRINGING EXTENSION ANNEX FOR DR. ANAND KRISHNAMURTHY

This springing extension applies solely to Dr. Krishnamurthy and shall be subject to the same customary exceptions and early release mechanics set forth in Exhibit A."}
