**Firm-Commitment Initial Public Offering of Common Stock of Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc.**

---

**Dated as of March 19, 2025**

---

Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc., a corporation duly organized and existing under the laws of the State of Delaware (the "Company"), the selling stockholders named in Schedule A hereto (collectively, the "Selling Stockholders," and each a "Selling Stockholder"), and Hargrove Securities LLC and Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc. (each an "Underwriter" and collectively, the "Underwriters"), for whom Hargrove Securities LLC and Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc. are acting as Representatives (in such capacity, the "Representatives"), hereby confirm their agreement with each other as follows:

## 1. Definitions

In addition to the terms defined elsewhere herein, the following terms have the meanings set forth in this Section 1:

"Act" means the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and the rules and regulations of the Commission thereunder.

"Additional Shares" means the shares of Common Stock, if any, to be purchased by the Underwriters upon exercise of the Over-Allotment Option pursuant to Section 2(b) hereof.

"Affiliate" has the meaning set forth in Rule 405 under the Act.

"Agreement" means this Underwriting Agreement, including all Schedules and Exhibits hereto.

"Applicable Time" means 5:00 p.m., New York City time, on the date hereof.

"Blue Sky" means the state securities or "blue sky" laws of the several states.

"Closing" means the closing of the purchase and sale of the Firm Shares pursuant to Section 6 hereof.

"Closing Date" means March 24, 2025, or such other date as the Representatives and the Company may agree upon in writing, being the third business day after the date hereof.

"Commission" means the United States Securities and Exchange Commission.

"Common Stock" means the common stock, par value $0.001 per share, of the Company.

"Company Free Writing Prospectus" means any "issuer free writing prospectus," as defined in Rule 433 under the Act, relating to the Offering.

"Exchange Act" means the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and the rules and regulations of the Commission thereunder.

"FINRA" means the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority, Inc.

"Firm Shares" means an aggregate of 12,000,000 shares of Common Stock, consisting of (i) 8,000,000 shares of Common Stock to be sold by the Company (the "Company Shares") and (ii) 4,000,000 shares of Common Stock to be sold by the Selling Stockholders (the "Selling Stockholder Shares"), in each case as set forth in Schedule A and Schedule B hereto.

"Free Writing Prospectus" means any "free writing prospectus" as defined in Rule 405 under the Act.

"GAAP" means United States generally accepted accounting principles.

"Initial Prospectus" means the preliminary prospectus included in the Registration Statement at the Applicable Time.

"Lock-Up Agreements" means the lock-up agreements, substantially in the form of Exhibit A hereto, executed and delivered by the Company's directors, officers, and holders of one percent (1%) or more of the outstanding Common Stock (on a pre-Offering basis) pursuant to Section 5(o) hereof.

"Over-Allotment Option" means the option granted to the Underwriters pursuant to Section 2(b) hereof to purchase up to 1,800,000 Additional Shares.

"Preliminary Prospectus" means the preliminary prospectus included in the Registration Statement, dated March 12, 2025.

"Prospectus" means the Final Prospectus (as defined below).

"Public Offering Price" means $24.00 per share, being the initial public offering price per share at which the Firm Shares (and any Additional Shares) are to be offered to the public, as agreed among the Company, the Selling Stockholders, and the Representatives on the date hereof.

"Registration Statement" means the registration statement on Form S-1 (File No. 333-284517), as amended, including all documents incorporated or deemed to be incorporated by reference therein, filed by the Company with the Commission under the Act, as declared effective by the Commission on March 12, 2025.

"Regulation M" means Regulation M under the Exchange Act.

"Required Filings" means the filings required to be made by the Company with the Commission and the states in connection with the Offering.

"Rule 158," "Rule 164," "Rule 172," "Rule 405," "Rule 415," "Rule 424," "Rule 430A," "Rule 433" and "Rule 5110" mean, in each case, the rule under the Act or the Exchange Act, as applicable, so designated.

"Final Prospectus" means the final prospectus relating to the Firm Shares (and any Additional Shares) filed by the Company with the Commission pursuant to Rule 424(b)(4) under the Act.

"Testing-the-Waters Communication" means any "testing-the-waters communication" as defined in Rule 405 under the Act.

"Time of Sale" means the time at which the Firm Shares were first sold to the public pursuant to the Offering.

"Time of Sale Prospectus" means the Preliminary Prospectus, together with any Issuer Free Writing Prospectus and any other free writing prospectus permitted under Rule 164(b) under the Act, in the form used at the Time of Sale.

## 2. Sale and Purchase of Firm Shares; Over-Allotment Option

### (a) Firm Shares.

Upon the terms and subject to the conditions herein set forth, the Company agrees to sell to the several Underwriters, and each of the Underwriters, upon the several and not joint representations, warranties, covenants, and agreements of the Company and the Selling Stockholders contained herein, agrees to purchase from the Company and the Selling Stockholders, severally and not jointly, at the purchase price per share equal to the Public Offering Price less the underwriting discount of six percent (6.0%) (i.e., $1.44 per share, resulting in a purchase price of $22.56 per share) (the "Purchase Price"), the respective number of Firm Shares set forth opposite each Underwriter's name in Schedule B hereto.

### (b) Over-Allotment Option.

In order to facilitate the distribution of the Firm Shares, the Company hereby grants to the Representatives, for the account of the several Underwriters, an option (the "Over-Allotment Option") to purchase, severally and not jointly, up to 1,800,000 Additional Shares (equal to fifteen percent (15%) of the Firm Shares) from the Company, at the Purchase Price, solely for the purpose of covering over-allotments, if any, in connection with the Offering. The Over-Allotment Option is exercisable in whole or in part at any time, and from time to time, within thirty (30) calendar days after the Closing Date (i.e., on or before April 23, 2025), upon written or telegraphic notice to the Company by or on behalf of the Representatives. Upon any exercise of the Over-Allotment Option, the Additional Shares shall be purchased by the Underwriters severally and not jointly, in the respective proportions set forth in Schedule B hereto. The Additional Shares shall be purchased by the Underwriters from the Company only; the Selling Stockholders shall have no obligation to sell any Additional Shares, and the Over-Allotment Option shall be sourced exclusively from newly issued shares of Common Stock.

### (c) Additional Closing.

Upon any exercise of the Over-Allotment Option, the closing of the purchase of the Additional Shares (the "Additional Closing") shall take place at the offices of Ashford & Pine LLP, 1231 Avenue of the Americas, 34th Floor, New York, New York 10020, or remotely via the facilities of The Depository Trust Company, on the third business day after the date of the notice of exercise (or such other date as the Representatives and the Company may agree upon in writing).

## 3. Representations and Warranties of the Company

The Company represents and warrants to each Underwriter, as of the date hereof, the Applicable Time, and the Closing Date (or such other date as is expressly indicated), as follows:

### (a) Organization and Good Standing.

The Company is a corporation duly organized, validly existing, and in good standing under the laws of the State of Delaware and is qualified to do business as a foreign corporation in each jurisdiction in which the nature of its business or the ownership or leasing of its properties makes such qualification necessary, except where the failure to be so qualified would not, individually or in the aggregate, have a Material Adverse Effect (as defined below). "Material Adverse Effect" means any material adverse change in the business, properties, management, financial condition, or results of operations of the Company and its subsidiaries, taken as a whole.

### (b) Capitalization.

The authorized capital stock of the Company consists of 200,000,000 shares of Common Stock, par value $0.001 per share, and 10,000,000 shares of preferred stock, par value $0.001 per share. As of the date hereof, 42,000,000 shares of Common Stock are issued and outstanding, and no shares of preferred stock are issued and outstanding. All outstanding shares of Common Stock have been duly authorized, validly issued, fully paid, and nonassessable, and were issued in compliance with applicable federal and state securities laws. The Company's Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation, effective as of December 20, 2024, and its Amended and Restated Bylaws are in the form filed as exhibits to the Registration Statement. As of December 31, 2024, there were outstanding under the Company's 2024 Equity Incentive Plan 4,200,000 stock options at a weighted-average exercise price of $6.50 per share, and 1,800,000 shares of Common Stock were reserved for future grants thereunder.

### (c) Authorization; Enforceability.

The Company has full corporate power and authority to execute and deliver this Agreement and to consummate the transactions contemplated hereby. This Agreement has been duly authorized, executed, and delivered by the Company and constitutes the valid and binding obligation of the Company, enforceable against it in accordance with its terms, except as enforceability may be limited by applicable bankruptcy, insolvency, and similar laws and by general principles of equity.

### (d) Validity of the Shares.

The Company Shares and the Additional Shares, when issued and delivered and paid for in accordance with this Agreement, will be duly authorized, validly issued, fully paid, and nonassessable, and will be free of any liens, encumbrances, equities, or claims created by the Company.

### (e) No Conflicts.

The execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement and the consummation of the transactions contemplated hereby will not (i) conflict with or constitute a breach or default under the Company's Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation or Amended and Restated Bylaws; (ii) conflict with or constitute a default under any material agreement or instrument to which the Company is a party or by which it is bound, including the supply agreement with Tanaka Precision Components Ltd., the exclusive license agreement with the University of Texas at Austin, and the revolving credit facility with Oakvale National Bank; or (iii) violate any applicable law or order, in each case except where the conflict, breach, default, or violation would not, individually or in the aggregate, have a Material Adverse Effect.

### (f) Registration Statement and Prospectus.

The Registration Statement is the most recent registration statement filed by the Company with the Commission for the registration of the Firm Shares (and any Additional Shares) under the Act. The Registration Statement has been declared effective by the Commission under the Act. The Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, and the Final Prospectus, in each case as of the respective dates thereof and as of the Closing Date, comply and will comply in all material respects with the requirements of the Act and the Exchange Act and the rules and regulations thereunder, and do not and will not contain any untrue statement of a material fact or omit to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading. The documents incorporated or deemed to be incorporated by reference in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, and the Final Prospectus, when such documents were filed with the Commission, conformed and will conform in all material respects to the requirements of the Exchange Act and the rules and regulations thereunder and did not and will not, when read together with the other statements in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, or the Final Prospectus, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, contain any untrue statement of a material fact or omit to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein not misleading.

### (g) Financial Statements.

The financial statements included in the Registration Statement, together with the related notes and schedules, present fairly, in all material respects, the financial position of the Company and its subsidiaries as of the dates and for the periods indicated, and the results of operations and cash flows of the Company and its subsidiaries for the periods indicated, and such financial statements have been prepared in conformity with GAAP applied on a consistent basis throughout the periods involved. The supporting schedules, if any, included in the Registration Statement present fairly, in all material respects, the information required to be stated therein. The audited financial statements included in the Registration Statement have been audited by Whitman Reese & Co., independent registered public accounting firm, in accordance with the standards of the Public Company Accounting Oversight Board (United States).

### (h) No Material Adverse Change.

Since the date of the most recent audited financial statements included in the Registration Statement (December 31, 2024), (i) there has been no Material Adverse Effect and (ii) there has been no loss, destruction, or material damage to the properties of the Company and its subsidiaries, whether or not covered by insurance, that would, individually or in the aggregate, have a Material Adverse Effect.

### (i) Intellectual Property.

The Company owns or possesses sufficient legal rights to all patents, patent applications, trademarks, service marks, trade names, copyrights, domain names, trade secrets, know-how, and other intellectual property necessary for the conduct of its business as currently conducted and as described in the Registration Statement, including fourteen (14) issued U.S. utility patents, seven (7) pending patent applications, and the exclusive license agreement with the University of Texas at Austin for bioelectric signal processing technology. The Company has not received any written notice of infringement, conflict, or claim alleging that any of the foregoing is invalid or unenforceable, except as disclosed in the Registration Statement.

### (j) Regulatory Compliance.

The Company is in compliance in all material respects with all applicable laws, rules, and regulations of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration, including the requirements applicable to the PulseGuard Pro cardiac rhythm monitoring device, which received FDA 510(k) clearance (Clearance No. K230847) on August 22, 2023.

### (k) Material Contracts.

Each material contract to which the Company is a party, including the supply agreement with Tanaka Precision Components Ltd. (effective through December 31, 2027, with a minimum annual purchase commitment of $12,000,000), the exclusive license agreement with the University of Texas at Austin (effective July 1, 2018, with a remaining term through June 30, 2033, and a royalty of 3.5% of net sales), and the revolving credit facility with Oakvale National Bank (aggregate commitment of $40,000,000, of which $22,500,000 was outstanding as of December 31, 2024), is in full force and effect, constitutes a legal, valid, and binding obligation of the Company, and the Company is not in material breach or default thereunder.

### (l) Litigation.

Except as disclosed in the Registration Statement, there is no action, suit, proceeding, or investigation pending or, to the knowledge of the Company, threatened against the Company before any court or governmental authority that, if adversely determined, would, individually or in the aggregate, have a Material Adverse Effect. The matters disclosed in the Registration Statement include (i) the patent infringement action brought by VitaBand Corp. (Case No. 6:24-cv-00891, U.S. District Court, Eastern District of Texas), in which VitaBand Corp. seeks monetary damages of $45,000,000, plus enhanced damages, attorneys' fees, costs, and permanent injunctive relief, and (ii) a charge of discrimination filed with the U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission on September 3, 2024, with respect to which the Company has established a reserve of $350,000.

### (m) Tax Matters.

The Company has filed all material federal, state, and foreign tax returns required to be filed and has paid all material taxes due, except as disclosed in the Registration Statement. As of December 31, 2024, the Company had approximately $14.2 million in federal net operating loss carryforwards, as disclosed in the Registration Statement.

### (n) FINRA Affiliations.

No officer, director, or holder of five percent (5%) or more of the Company's securities (calculated on a pre-Offering basis) is, or within the 180 days prior to the initial filing of the Registration Statement has been, an affiliate or associated person of any FINRA member, except as disclosed in the Registration Statement or in the selling stockholder questionnaires delivered to the Representatives and Underwriters' Counsel.

### (o) Compliance with Securities Laws.

The Company has complied, and will comply, in all material respects with all applicable federal and state securities laws in connection with the Offering.

### (p) Nasdaq Listing.

The Firm Shares (and any Additional Shares) have been approved for listing on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the symbol "MPLS," subject to official notice of issuance.

### (q) No Stabilization.

The Company has not taken, and will not take, directly or indirectly, any action designed to cause or result in, or that would reasonably be expected to cause or result in, the stabilization or manipulation of the price of the Common Stock to facilitate the sale or resale of the Firm Shares (or any Additional Shares).

### (r) Independent Auditor.

Whitman Reese & Co., which has certified certain financial statements included in the Registration Statement, is an independent registered public accounting firm with respect to the Company within the meaning of the Act and the rules and regulations of the Commission and the Public Company Accounting Oversight Board.

### (s) Use of Proceeds.

The use of proceeds from the sale of the Company Shares, as described in the Registration Statement under "Use of Proceeds," does not and will not violate the provisions of Regulation T, U, or X of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System or any other applicable law.

## 8. Indemnification and Contribution

### (a) Indemnification by the Company.

The Company shall indemnify and hold harmless each Underwriter, its affiliates, and each person who controls such Underwriter within the meaning of Section 15 of the Act or Section 20 of the Exchange Act, against any and all losses, claims, damages, and liabilities (including reasonable costs of investigation and defense), joint or several, to which such indemnified party may become subject, arising out of or based upon any untrue statement or alleged untrue statement of a material fact contained in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, the Final Prospectus, any Issuer Free Writing Prospectus, or any amendment or supplement thereto, or any omission or alleged omission to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading; provided, however, that the Company shall not be liable to the extent that any such loss, claim, damage, or liability arises out of or is based upon any untrue statement or omission made in reliance upon and in conformity with written information furnished to the Company by or on behalf of any Underwriter specifically for use in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, the Final Prospectus, or any amendment or supplement thereto.

### (b) Indemnification by the Selling Stockholders.

Each Selling Stockholder shall, severally and not jointly, indemnify and hold harmless each Underwriter, its affiliates, and each person who controls such Underwriter within the meaning of Section 15 of the Act or Section 20 of the Exchange Act, against any and all losses, claims, damages, and liabilities to which such indemnified party may become subject, arising out of or based upon any untrue statement or alleged untrue statement of a material fact contained in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, the Final Prospectus, or any amendment or supplement thereto, or any omission or alleged omission to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading, but only with respect to the Selling Stockholder Information furnished in writing by such Selling Stockholder specifically for inclusion therein. Each Selling Stockholder's liability under this Section 8(b), whether for indemnification or contribution, shall not exceed the net proceeds actually received by such Selling Stockholder from the sale of its Selling Stockholder Shares in the Offering.

### (c) Indemnification by the Underwriters.

Each Underwriter shall, severally and not jointly, indemnify and hold harmless the Company, its directors, each officer of the Company who signed the Registration Statement, each person who controls the Company within the meaning of Section 15 of the Act or Section 20 of the Exchange Act, and each Selling Stockholder, against any and all losses, claims, damages, and liabilities arising out of or based upon any untrue statement or alleged untrue statement of a material fact contained in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, the Final Prospectus, or any amendment or supplement thereto, or any omission or alleged omission to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading, but only to the extent that such untrue statement or omission was made in reliance upon and in conformity with written information furnished to the Company by or on behalf of such Underwriter specifically for use therein.

### (d) Contribution.

If the indemnification provided for in this Section 8 is unavailable or insufficient to hold harmless an indemnified party, then each indemnifying party shall contribute to the aggregate amount of losses, claims, damages, and liabilities to which such indemnified party may be subject (i) in such proportion as is appropriate to reflect the relative benefits received by the Company, the Selling Stockholders, and the Underwriters from the Offering, or (ii) if such allocation is not permitted by applicable law, in such proportion as is appropriate to reflect not only such relative benefits but also the relative fault of the Company, the Selling Stockholders, and the Underwriters. The aggregate contribution of the Underwriters shall not exceed the total underwriting discount actually received by the Underwriters. No Selling Stockholder shall be required to contribute an amount in excess of the net proceeds received by such Selling Stockholder from the sale of its Selling Stockholder Shares in the Offering. No person found guilty of fraudulent misrepresentation within the meaning of Section 11(f) of the Act shall be entitled to contribution from any person who was not guilty of such fraudulent misrepresentation.

### (e) Procedure.

Promptly after receipt by an indemnified party of notice of the commencement of any action, such indemnified party shall, if a claim in respect thereof is to be made against an indemnifying party, notify the indemnifying party in writing; provided, however, that the failure to so notify shall not relieve the indemnifying party from any liability hereunder except to the extent it is materially prejudiced thereby. The indemnifying party shall be entitled to participate in, and, to the extent it shall desire, to assume the defense of any such action with counsel satisfactory to the indemnified party. The indemnified party shall have the right to employ separate counsel in any such action and to participate in the defense thereof, the fees and expenses of which shall be borne by the indemnified party unless the employment of such counsel has been authorized in writing by the indemnifying party in connection with the defense of such action or the indemnifying party has not employed counsel within a reasonable time after receiving notice of the action.

## 9. Termination

The Representatives may terminate this Agreement at any time prior to the Closing Date by written notice to the Company and the Selling Stockholders if, in the judgment of the Representatives, any of the following has occurred:

### (a) Material Adverse Change.

Any Material Adverse Change, or any event that, in the reasonable judgment of the Representatives, makes it impracticable or inadvisable to proceed with the Offering.

### (b) Market Disruption.

Any suspension or material limitation of trading in securities generally on the Nasdaq Stock Market, the New York Stock Exchange, or any other national securities exchange.

### (c) Banking Moratorium.

Any general moratorium on commercial banking activities declared by federal or New York state authorities.

### (d) Settlement Disruption.

Any material disruption in securities settlement, payment, or clearance services in the United States.

### (e) National Emergency.

Any outbreak or escalation of hostilities, declaration of war, national emergency, act of terrorism, or other calamity or crisis that, in the reasonable judgment of the Representatives, materially and adversely affects United States financial markets.

### (f) Rating Downgrade.

Any downgrade, or public notice of any intended or potential downgrade, of the rating of any securities of the Company or any of its subsidiaries by any nationally recognized statistical rating organization.

Upon any such termination, no party shall have any liability to any other party except (i) for expenses as provided in Section 10(g) and (ii) the indemnification and contribution provisions of Section 8, which shall survive any such termination.

**Holders of 1% or More of Outstanding Common Stock (pre-Offering):**

- Cascade Kestridge Ventures, LP (6,200,000 shares; 14.76%)
- Northlight Growth Partners Fund II, LP (4,800,000 shares; 11.43%)

## SCHEDULE D — OFFICERS SIGNING THE REGISTRATION STATEMENT

The officers of the Company who signed the Registration Statement on Form S-1 (File No. 333-284517) are:

- Dr. Anand Krishnamurthy — Chairman and Chief Executive Officer
- David Nishimura — Chief Financial Officer

## EXHIBIT A — FORM OF LOCK-UP AGREEMENT

[The form of Lock-Up Agreement is substantially in the form of the Lock-Up Agreement dated as of March __, 2025, among each locked-up party and Hargrove Securities LLC and Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc., as Representatives, providing for a lock-up period of one hundred eighty (180) days from the date of the Final Prospectus, with the customary exceptions set forth therein, including: (a) bona fide gifts and charitable contributions; (b) transfers to immediate family members or estate planning trusts; (c) transfers by operation of law, including pursuant to a qualified domestic relations order, divorce decree, or intestacy; (d) transfers to affiliates, limited partners, members, or stockholders of the locked-up party; (e) transactions pursuant to a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan established prior to the date of the Underwriting Agreement; (f) the exercise of stock options or settlement of restricted stock units (with the underlying shares remaining subject to the lock-up), including sell-to-cover transactions solely to satisfy tax withholding obligations; (g) the sale of shares pursuant to the Underwriting Agreement by a Selling Stockholder; and (h) transfers to the Company in connection with repurchases upon termination of employment or net exercise of options. In each case, the transferee must agree in writing to be bound by the terms of the Lock-Up Agreement.

Hargrove Securities LLC may, in its sole discretion, release any or all of the Lock-Up Securities at any time. If Hargrove Securities LLC releases Lock-Up Securities representing, in the aggregate, more than one percent (1%) of the Company's then-outstanding shares of Common Stock, Hargrove Securities LLC shall provide the Company with at least three (3) business days' prior written notice of such release, and the Company shall promptly announce such release by press release or on Form 8-K.]

## EXHIBIT B — SPRINGING EXTENSION ANNEX FOR DR. ANAND KRISHNAMURTHY

In addition to the standard 180-day lock-up set forth in Exhibit A, the Lock-Up Agreement executed by Dr. Anand Krishnamurthy shall include the following springing extension provision:

If the closing price of the Common Stock on the Nasdaq Global Select Market is below the Public Offering Price per share ($24.00) for five (5) consecutive trading days during the final seventeen (17) trading days of the standard 180-day lock-up period, then Dr. Krishnamurthy's lock-up shall automatically extend by an additional eighteen (18) calendar days beyond the standard 180-day period, for a total lock-up of one hundred ninety-eight (198) days from the date of the Final Prospectus. This springing extension applies solely to Dr. Krishnamurthy and shall be subject to the same customary exceptions and early release mechanics set forth in Exhibit A."}
